ref: After Jesus left, he went to Judea and then on to the other side of the Jordan River. Once again large crowds came to him, and as usual, he taught them.
text: Zezi laan ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ o vu o da Zude tɩ-niǝ yam. O ma vu o bɛ Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam ni dɩdoŋ dɩm nɩ. Nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan daa ma ba ba gilim-o. O ma ta brɩ-ba Wɛ cɩga kam, nɩ o na yǝni o kɩ te tɩn.
translated: He said he saw a large snake in his farm. He said he killed the snake with a stick. He was very scared. He told his son Wɛ to be careful, in case he encountered it again.

ref: He becomes like one person with his wife. Then they are no longer two people, but one.
text: Kʋntʋ wʋ́ pa ba laan ji nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa. Ba daa daɩ nɔɔna bale, ba laan yɩ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ.
translated: The man's father has a lot of cows. He gave his son many cows, he has a lot of cows.

ref: The disciples were shocked to hear this. So Jesus told them again, “It's terribly hard to get into God's kingdom!
text: O karabiǝ bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-ba. Zezi daa ta ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A biǝ-ba, nɔɔna na lagɩ sɩ ba zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ, kʋʋ́ ta tiini kʋ cana kʋ pa-ba.
translated: When the crab saw that the tortoise was coming, it hid. The tortoise came and said to it: "My friend, I have seen your footprints leading to your house, but I did not see you coming out."

ref: Many people told the man to stop, but he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
text: Nɔɔna zanzan ma bag-o sɩ o pu o ni. O laan ma tiini o kaasɩ zanzan yɩ o wɩ: «Pɛ Davidi dwi tu, duri a yibwǝnǝ!»
translated: The old woman went to the farm and saw a very big tree. She shouted and said: "Oh David, come and see this huge tree!"

ref: After Jesus had gone to Jerusalem, he went into the temple and looked around at everything. But since it was already late in the day, he went back to Bethany with the twelve disciples. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Matthew
text: Zezi ma vu o zʋ Zeruzalɛm tɩʋ kʋm wʋ. O ma vu o yi Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm o zʋ kʋ wʋ. O ma kaagɩ o nii wǝǝnu tɩm maama na yɩ te. Wɛ na lagɩ dɩ zʋ tɩn, o ma joori o nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ, o tɔgɩ dɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam o vu Betani. Zezi na sɔɔlɩ kapʋrʋ te tɩn (Matiyu
translated: Jesus went to Jerusalem as was his custom. He also went to the temple in Jerusalem. He saw a fig tree but it had no fruit. When he saw this, he cursed the fig tree and it withered. He then went to Bethany. This is mentioned in Matthew.

ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, say, ‘The Lord needs it and will soon bring it back.’ ”
text: Nɔɔnʋ na bwe abam nɩ: ‹Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á bwǝli-ka?›, sɩ á ta dɩd-o nɩ: ‹Kʋ yɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm mʋ laga. Oó joori o pa ka ba lele.› »
translated: He asked his father: "Why are you crying?", and he replied: "I am mourning for Yuutu. He was a good man who did not deserve to die."

ref: The disciples led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back, and Jesus got on.
text: Ba ma ja bɩna-pɔla kam ba ja vu ba yi Zezi te. Ba laan ma kwe ba gwaarʋ ba daŋɩ ka baŋa nɩ. Zezi ma di ka baŋa, yɩ ba ve ba maa kɛa.
translated: They said they would not believe unless they saw Jesus themselves. They insisted that they would have to see the wounds in his hands. Jesus appeared to them, showing them his hands.

ref: more to the birth of the Messiah. The Birth of Jesus (Luke
text: Azori ma lʋ Sadoki. Sadoki ma lʋ Akimi. Akimi ma lʋ Eliyudi.
translated: Azor was the father of Sadok. Sadok was the father of Akim. Akim was the father of Elihud.

ref: While Joseph was thinking about this, an angel from the Lord appeared to him in a dream. The angel said, “Joseph, the baby that Mary will have is from the Holy Spirit. Go ahead and marry her.
text: O na bʋŋɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, Baŋa-Wɛ malɛka ma ba o te o dɩndwɩa wʋnɩ, yɩ ka ta dɩd-o ka wɩ: «Zʋzɛfʋ, Pɛ Davidi dwi tu, yɩ pa n wʋ cɔgɩ sɩ n joŋi Mari n ma n ji n kaanɩ. Bɛŋwaanɩ, o na jɩgɩ pugǝ kam tɩn, kʋ yɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm dam ŋwaanɩ mʋ.
translated: As he was thinking about this, the angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph, son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary as your wife, because what is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.

ref: and said, “Where is the child born to be king of the Jews? We saw his star in the east and have come to worship him.”
text: Ba laan ma bwe nɔɔna ba wɩ: «Bu wʋm ba na lʋgɩ sɩ o ji Zwifǝ pɛ tɩn wʋ yǝn mʋ? Bɛŋwaanɩ dɩbam nɛ calɩcʋa kalʋ na brɩ o lʋra kam tɩn na nuŋi bri-kwaga seeni, yɩ dɩbam ba sɩ dɩ́ zul-o.»
translated: They asked him: "Why do you speak to the people in parables? Because it has been given to you to know the secrets of the kingdom of heaven, but to them it has not been given."

ref: So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet Jeremiah had said,
text: Biǝ bam gʋm dɩm ma pa Wɛ nijoŋnu Zeremi na tagɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kalʋ faŋa faŋa tɩn sɩɩnɩ kʋ kɩ.
translated: We went together to see Jeremiah and then we sat under the big tree and talked for a long time.

ref: After King Herod died, an angel from the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph while he was still in Egypt.
text: Erɔdɩ dɛɛn ma ba o tɩ, yɩ Zʋzɛfʋ daa ta wʋ Ezipi nɩ. Baŋa-Wɛ malɛka ma daa ba Zʋzɛfʋ te o dɩndwɩa wʋnɩ, yɩ ka wɩ:
translated: After Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt and said to him:

ref: John wore clothes made of camel's hair. He had a leather strap around his waist and ate grasshoppers and wild honey.
text: Zan dɛɛn zʋʋrɩ gɔrɔ kʋlʋ ba na sɔgɩ dɩ yogondi kʋrʋ tɩn mʋ. O maa maɩ kilǝ o ma ja o tǝŋǝ. O wʋdiu mʋ yɩ kayɩra dɩ tʋʋrʋ.
translated: The man went to the farm early in the morning and worked there until evening. He was very tired when he returned home. He sat down to rest for a while.

ref: They told how sorry they were for their sins, and he baptized them in the river.
text: Ba maa tɛ ba brɩ kʋlʋ ba na kɩ ba cɔgɩ tɩn. O laan maa paɩ ba tu Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam wʋnɩ, yɩ o miisi-ba na wʋnɩ Wɛ ŋwaanɩ.
translated: If they do not come early, they will not find food to eat. That is why they always leave Zurdɛn early in the morning, so that they can reach Wen before sunrise.

ref: Finally, the devil took Jesus up on a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms on earth and their power.
text: Sʋtaanɩ daa ma ja-o dɩ ja vu dɩ di piu kʋlʋ na tiini kʋ dwara tɩn yuu. Dɩ laan ma brɩ-o lʋgʋ baŋa tɩɩnɩ dɩm maama dɩ wo-laarʋ tɩlʋ maama na wʋra tɩn.
translated: The teacher said that if you want to go to the farm, you should first ask your parents for permission. You must also take along enough food and water so that you do not get hungry or thirsty on the way.

ref: “Listen, lands of Zebulun and Naphtali, lands along the road to the sea and across the Jordan. Listen Galilee, land of the Gentiles!
text: O dɛɛn tagɩ o wɩ: «Kʋ na yɩ Zabulɔn dɩ Nɛfitali tɩɩnɩ dɩm, dɩ je sɩlʋ na wʋ nɩnɩʋ kʋm seeni tɩn, dɩ je sɩlʋ na wʋ Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam bubɛ dɩdoŋ dɩm kwaga nɩ tɩn, dɩ Galile je sɩlʋ dwi-gɛ tiinǝ na zʋʋrɩ da tɩn,
translated: He said this so that it would be fulfilled: "Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali, the way of the sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles,"

ref: While Jesus was walking along the shore of Lake Galilee, he saw two brothers. One was Simon, also known as Peter, and the other was Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting their net into the lake.
text: Zezi dɛɛn maa wʋ Galile nɩnɩʋ kʋm ni nɩ o veǝ. O ma na nɔɔna bale, ba yɩ cʋrʋ mʋ. Ba dɩdʋa yɩrɩ mʋ Simɔn, o yɩrɩ dɩdoŋ maa yɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ. Wʋdoŋ wʋm maa yɩ o nyaanɩ, o yɩrɩ mʋ Andre. Ba yɩ kalǝŋ-jara mʋ, yɩ ba wʋ na bam wʋnɩ ba dʋlɩ bʋrɩ ba ma jaanɩ kale.
translated: Jesus went to Galilee and saw some people there. He saw two brothers, one was called Simon, who is also called Peter. His brother was named Andrew. They were fishermen, and they were casting a net into the lake because they were fishermen.

ref: God blesses those people who grieve. They will find comfort!
text: Balʋ wʋrʋ na cɔgɩ tɩn mʋ jɩgɩ yu-yoŋo, Wɛ na wʋ́ pa-ba wʋ-zuru tɩn ŋwaanɩ.
translated: When you go to the farm in the morning, you should greet the elders you meet.

ref: and no one lights a lamp and puts it under a clay pot. Instead, it is placed on a lampstand, where it can give light to everyone in the house.
text: Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ bá sɛ o tarɩgɩ kanɩa o pu tɩtɔgɔ kuri nɩ. Oó kwe-ka o palɩ wɛɛnɩ mʋ, sɩ ka pooni dɩm taa naɩ dɩ paɩ nɔɔna balʋ maama na wʋ digǝ kam wʋnɩ tɩn.
translated: Long ago, a man went to the farm with his son. When they reached the bush, they saw a big snake lying on the path, so they stopped and thought about what to do before they continued on their way.

ref: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
text: Kʋ daarɩ, a nan lagɩ a ta dɩ abam nɩ, nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ ban-zɔŋɔ dɩ o ko-bu, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ ba di kʋntʋ tu dɩ taanɩ mʋ. Wʋlʋ na tagɩ dɩ o ko-bu o wɩ, o ba jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋa, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ dɩbam sarɩya-dirǝ nakwa bam mʋ di kʋntʋ tu taanɩ. Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ dɩ na tagɩ dɩ o ko-bu o wɩ, o yɩ joro, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ kʋntʋ tu zʋ min-tɩʋ mʋ.
translated: If you do that, and someone tells you not to do it again, but you still go ahead and repeat the same thing, you are not respecting their words. If someone repeats the same thing to you, and you do not listen, you are not showing respect, and people will say you are not a good person. But if someone repeats the same thing to you and you listen, you are wise, and people will say you are a good person.

ref: You have been taught that a man who divorces his wife must write out divorce papers for her.
text: «Ba maŋɩ ba ta ba wɩ: ‹Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ na yagɩ o kaanɩ, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o pa-o tɔnɔ kʋlʋ na wʋ́ brɩ nɩ ba yagɩ daanɩ tɩn.›
translated: They said to him: ‘If you want to go to the farm, you must first ask your father before you go so that he will know where you are going.’

ref: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
text: «Abam maŋɩ á ni sɩ ba tagɩ ba wɩ: ‹Nɔɔnʋ na pi n yi, sɩ ba pi kʋntʋ tu dɩ yi. Nɔɔnʋ nan na guri n yǝli, sɩ ba guri kʋntʋ tu dɩ yǝli.›
translated: The elders said to them: ‘Let us do as we say, so that we do not bring trouble upon ourselves. Let us do as we have agreed, so that we do not bring trouble upon ourselves.’

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Abam nan na soe balʋ dɩ na soe abam tɩn yɩranɩ, bɛɛ nyɔɔrɩ mʋ á wʋ́ na Wɛ tee nɩ? Lampo-joŋnǝ dɩ yǝni ba kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ.
translated: If you do something bad to someone and the person does something bad to you in return, is it good or bad? The elders say it is not good to repay evil with evil.

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Abam nan na zuli á cʋrrʋ yɩranɩ mʋ, abam kɩ á dwe á donnǝ bam na? Balʋ na yǝri Wɛ tɩn dɩ nan yǝni ba kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ.
translated: If someone gives you something good, and you don't thank the person, what does that mean? It means you don't value what the person did for you.

ref: But if you don't forgive others, your Father will not forgive your sins. Worshiping God by Going without Eating
text: Kʋ daarɩ, abam na wʋ yagɩ á ma cɛ balʋ na kɩ abam lwarɩm tɩn, á Ko Wɛ dɩ bá yagɩ abam lwarɩm DƖ ma cɛ abam.
translated: If you agree, then you will give me the money and I will give you the goods, but if you do not give me the goods, I will not give you the money.

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Abam nan maŋɩ sɩ á tiŋi á jɩjɩgɩrʋ zanzan Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ mʋ. Dáanɩ mʋ tʋa daa bá wanɩ ka mu-tɩ, yɩ tɩ bá sʋŋɩ tɩ cɔgɩ, yɩ ŋwɩɩna dɩ bá wanɩ ba ŋɔ-tɩ.
translated: My father said that we should go and greet the chief of We-songon. If we do not go today, he will be angry with us, so we must go and greet him before he gets angry.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: Bɛŋwaanɩ, abam jɩjɩgɩrʋ na tigi mɛ tɩn, á bɩcara dɩ tigi dáanɩ mʋ.
translated: Yesterday, the child went to the farm with his father, but he did not return with him.

ref: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
text: Nmʋ yiǝ nan na ba lana, kʋ paɩ n yɩra maama wʋ lim wʋnɩ mʋ. Kʋntʋ tɩn, nmʋ yiǝ ya na maŋɩ sɩ ya taa yɩ pooni tɩn nan na yɩ lim, kʋ laan wʋ́ ta tiini kʋ yɩ lim kʋ ja gaalɩ.
translated: If you do this to your friend, he will not be happy with you. Also, if you do not want others to do the same thing to you, you should not do it to others either.

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Nan maanɩ-na á nii zunǝ na yɩ te tɩn. Bantʋ ba duǝ, yɩ ba ba zagɩ mɩna ba kɩ tuli nɩ. Kʋ nan yɩ á Ko Wɛ mʋ nii ba baŋa nɩ dɩ ba ni-wʋdiu. Abam nan ba jɩgɩ kuri á dwe zunǝ bam na?
translated: That person said he will come here tomorrow. If they arrive, we will all sit together and talk. But if he goes to Ko We and they do not return, we will wait for them. So when will they finish the work he talked about?

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Balʋ na yǝri Wɛ tɩn mʋ tiini ba bʋŋɩ wǝǝnu tɩm kʋntʋ maama wʋbʋŋa. Abam Ko Wɛ nan maŋɩ DƖ ye woŋo kʋlʋ na maŋɩ dɩ abam tɩn.
translated: If you go to Wɛ and you do not see the chief, you should greet his mother. Because in Wɛ, the mother is the one who gives birth to the chief.

ref: Don't condemn others, and God won't condemn you.
text: «Abam yɩ taá ŋɔɔnɩ á donnǝ wǝǝnu, sɩ Wɛ yɩ ŋɔɔnɩ abam dɩ wǝǝnu.
translated: If you do not go to the farm today, then I will go to the farm alone.

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: Á taá kɩ á donnǝ lanyɩranɩ, sɩ kʋ maŋɩ dɩ á na lagɩ sɩ bantʋ taa kɩ abam te tɩn. Kʋntʋ mʋ yɩ cɩga kalʋ Moyisi dɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam zaasɩm dɩm na maŋɩ dɩ brɩ tɩn.» Zezi karabiǝ na wʋ́ tɔgɩ cwǝŋǝ kalʋ tɩn (Luki
translated: “No one, after lighting a lamp, covers it with a jar or puts it under a bed. Instead, they put it on a stand, so that those who come in may see the light. For nothing is hidden that will not be disclosed, nor anything concealed that will not be known or brought out into the open.” Jesus said this to the people (Luke).

ref: But the gate to life is very narrow. The road that leads there is so hard to follow that only a few people find it. A Tree and Its Fruit (Luke
text: Kʋ daarɩ cwǝŋǝ kalʋ nmʋ na wʋ́ da sɩ n vu n joŋi ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ tɩn ni yɩ mɩmɩna mʋ, yɩ ka pɩɩnɩ. Nɔɔna fɩnfɩɩn yɩranɩ mʋ tɔgɩ cwǝŋǝ kam kʋntʋ.» Ba lwarɩ tiu kʋ biǝ lǝrim nɩ mʋ (Luki
translated: If someone slaps you on one cheek, turn to them the other also. If someone takes your coat, do not withhold your shirt from them. Give to everyone who asks you, and if anyone takes what belongs to you, do not demand it back. (Luke)

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Tiu kʋlʋ maama na ba lǝri bu-ŋʋna tɩn, baá go-kʋ ba dɩ mini nɩ mʋ.
translated: If your mother tells you to do something, you should listen to her and do it.

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.
text: Tɩga na tu ka wʋra ka yi tɩn, nɔɔna maa jaanɩ ciciri na jɩgɩ balʋ tɩn zanzan ba tui Zezi te. O maa yǝni o ma o ni-taanɩ o zǝli ciciri sɩm, yɩ o daarɩ o pa yawɩɩna bam maama na yazurǝ.
translated: If you do not forgive others their sins, your Father will not forgive your sins either. But if you forgive others, your Father in heaven will also forgive you.

ref: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
text: Vu-fɔrɔ ma da kʋ zaŋɩ kʋ magɩ boro kʋm, yɩ kʋ wʋra kʋ miisi na bam kuri nɩ, dɩ Zezi ya tigi o dɔa.
translated: The shepherd lays down his life for the sheep, just as Jesus gave his life for us.

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: Kʋ ma tiini kʋ kɩ nɔɔna bam yǝǝu, yɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ yɩ wɔɔ mʋ, yɩ viu dɩ na dɩ ta sɛ o ni?» Zezi na zǝli ciciri nɔɔna bale yɩra nɩ te tɩn (Marɩkɩ
translated: He was still speaking when some people arrived and said, “Teacher, this girl is dead, why are you still bothering him?” But Jesus ignored what they said and told the official, “Do not be afraid, only believe.” (Mark)

ref: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
text: O ma ta o wɩ, sɩ ve. Sɩ ma sɩɩnɩ sɩ nuŋi nɔɔna bam yɩra nɩ sɩ vu sɩ zʋ tǝrǝ bam. Tǝrǝ bam maama ma duri ba tu ba tʋ nɩnɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ ba li na ba tɩ.
translated: If you go, you will see. You will find things that will help you and you will know what to do. What to do is not always easy but if you are patient and work hard you will succeed.

ref: As Jesus was leaving that place, two blind men began following him and shouting, “Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Zezi dɛɛn ma zaŋɩ o maa kɛa. O na maa ve tɩn, lilwǝ bale ma zaŋɩ ba tɔgɩ o kwaga, yɩ ba tɛ dɩ kwǝr-dɩa ba wɩ: «Pɛ Davidi dwi tu! Duri dɩbam yibwǝnǝ!»
translated: Jesus was passing by on the road. As he was going, some people passing by heard him coming, and they began to shout loudly: "Son of David, have mercy on us! Help us!"

ref: Later, Jesus and his disciples were having dinner at Matthew's house. Many tax collectors and other sinners were also there.
text: Zezi dɛɛn ma vu Matiyu sɔŋɔ sɩ o di wʋdiu, o dɩ o karabiǝ bam. Lampo-joŋnǝ dɩ nɔn-cɩcɔgɩrʋ zanzan dɩ maa tɔgɩ ba wʋra ba di wʋdiu kʋm dɩ ba.
translated: Jesus went to where Matthew was sitting, he told him to follow him. Immediately he got up and followed him. Many tax collectors and sinners came and ate with Jesus and his disciples.

ref: Jesus turned. He saw the woman and said, “Don't worry! You are now healed because of your faith.” At that moment she was healed.
text: O na dwe-o kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma pipiri o nii-o yɩ o wɩ: «A bukɔ, kɩ pu-dɩa. Nmʋ na kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ te tɩn mʋ pɛ n na yazurǝ.» Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ nɔ́ɔ mʋ kaanɩ wʋm sɩɩnɩ o na yazurǝ.
translated: He called his son and said to him, “My child, come here. I want to tell you something before I die.” After the son came close, he told him what he wanted to say.

ref: Some followers of John the Baptist came and asked Jesus, “Why do we and the Pharisees often go without eating, while your disciples never do?”
text: Zan karabiǝ dɛɛn ma vu Zezi te, yɩ ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Dɩbam dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ bam yǝni dɩ́ vɔ ni maŋa zanzan. Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ nmʋ karabiǝ bam ba vɔ ni kʋntʋ?»
translated: Some of John's disciples came to Jesus and asked him, "Why is it that we and the Pharisees fast often, but your disciples do not fast?"

ref: he said, “Get out of here! The little girl isn't dead. She is just asleep.” Everyone started laughing at Jesus.

text: Nmʋ yi nan na wʋ́ pa n yagɩ Wɛ cwǝŋǝ tɔgɩm, kʋ maŋɩ sɩ n lɩ-dɩ n dʋlɩ n yagɩ. N na jɩgɩ yi dɩdʋa yɩ n na Wɛ ŋwɩa kam, kʋ garɩ n na jɩgɩ yiǝ yale, yɩ ba laan dɩ-m ba yagɩ mini dɩlʋ na bá dwe tɩn wʋnɩ.» Pi-jiǝ mɩmaŋa (Luki
translated: And he said to them, “When you pray, say: Father, may your name be kept holy. May your kingdom come. Give us each day our daily bread. And forgive us our sins, for we ourselves forgive everyone who is indebted to us. And do not lead us into temptation.” Luke

ref: Don't you think you should show pity to someone else, as I did to you?”
text: Kʋ ya maŋɩ sɩ nmʋ dɩ duri n doŋ wʋm ŋwaŋa, nɩnɛɛnɩ a na duri nmʋ ŋwaŋa te tɩn.›
translated: If you do not put water in the pot and place it on the fire, it is the pot that will burn, not the water.

ref: But if you are as humble as this child, you are the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
text: Wʋlʋ na dwe maama Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ tɩn mʋ yɩ wʋlʋ na tu o tɩtɩ o ji nɩnɛɛnɩ bu-balaŋa kantʋ doŋ te tɩn.
translated: The child and his mother went to the farm but when they reached there, the child said he was tired and could not work.

ref: And when you welcome one of these children because of me, you welcome me. Temptations To Sin (Mark
text: Kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, wʋlʋ maama na nɩgɩ bu wʋntʋ doŋ lanyɩranɩ amʋ yɩrɩ ŋwaanɩ tɩn, kʋntʋ tu nɩgɩ amʋ mʋ.
translated: If you want to go there, you must first ask your mother for permission, because she knows what is good for you.

ref: If one of my followers sins against you, go and point out what was wrong. But do it in private, just between the two of you. If that person listens, you have won back a follower.
text: «Nmʋ ko-bu na kɩ-m lwarɩm, sɩ n vu n kaan-o dɩ o na kɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, sɩ kʋ taa yɩ abam yɩranɩ taanɩ, sɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ ta tɔgɩ o tǝrǝ. O nan na sɛ nmʋ na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn, kʋ brɩ nɩ n joori n dɩ-o Wɛ cwǝŋǝ kam wʋnɩ mʋ.
translated: If you want to do good, but you do not have the means, and you do not have strength or knowledge, and you are not able to help your fellow human beings, do not let your heart be troubled. It is enough that your intention is good, for God knows what is in your heart.

ref: The official got down on his knees and began begging, “Have pity on me, and I will pay you every cent I owe!”
text: Tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma vu o kuni doonǝ pɛ wʋm yigǝ nɩ o loor-o o wɩ: ‹Amʋ tu, popo ja n banɩ dɩ amʋ, sɩ amʋ wʋ́ ŋwɩ n jɩnɩ dɩm maama a pa-m.›
translated: The woman took the child and went home, and when she arrived, she called her husband and said: ‘My husband, look at what I have brought for you, and I want you to take good care of this child for me.’

ref: But the first official refused to have pity. Instead, he went and had the other official put in jail until he could pay what he owed.
text: O ma wʋ sɛ, yɩ o ja nɔɔnʋ wʋm o vu o kɩ pɩɩna digǝ nɩ, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi maŋa kam o na wʋ́ ŋwɩ o jɩnɩ dɩm tɩn.
translated: If you do that, and you don't do it well, it will not bring the good results you want, and you will not be happy with what you get in the end.

ref: The king called the first official back in and said, “You're an evil man! When you begged for mercy, I said you did not have to pay back a cent.
text: Pɛ wʋm laan ma bǝŋi o tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ o ta dɩd-o o wɩ: ‹Nmʋ yɩ nɔn-balɔrɔ mʋ. Amʋ yagɩ nmʋ jɩ-farɩ dɩm maama mʋ a ma cɛ-m, nmʋ na loori-nɩ tɩn ŋwaanɩ.
translated: When the child finished eating his food, his mother said to him: ‘You are a good child. If you always eat your food like this, you will grow up to be strong.’

ref: Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you, it's terribly hard for rich people to get into the kingdom of heaven!
text: Zezi laan ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nadum na lagɩ sɩ o zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋnɩ, kʋʋ́ ta tiini kʋ cana kʋ pa-o.
translated: Jesus answered and said to him: "If I do not wash you, you have no part with me; unless I wash you, you cannot be clean."

ref: Jesus looked straight at them and said, “There are some things people cannot do, but God can do anything.”
text: Zezi ma nii-ba yɩ o wɩ: «Nabiinǝ mʋ bá wanɩ ba kɩ, sɩ Wɛ waɩ woŋo maama DƖ kɩa.»
translated: Jesus said to them: "A prophet is not accepted in his own hometown, except in his own country."

ref: He promised to pay them what was fair, if they would work in his vineyard.
text: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: ‹Abam dɩ ba á tɔgɩ á tʋŋɩ a kara kam nɩ, sɩ amʋ wʋ́ ŋwɩ abam te na maŋɩ tɩn.›
translated: He said to them: ‘If you have faith as small as a mustard seed, you can say to this tree, “Be uprooted and planted in the sea,” and it will obey you.’

ref: They said, “The ones who were hired last worked for only one hour. But you paid them the same that you did us. And we worked in the hot sun all day long!”
text: ‹Balʋ na tu kwaga tɩn tʋŋɩ fɩnfɩɩn yɩranɩ mʋ, nɩnɛɛnɩ luu dɩdʋa te. Dɩbam nan tu dɩ́ tʋŋɩ tɩtɩɩtɩ mʋ, kʋ cɛ dɩ́ yɩra wɩa yigǝ nɩ dɛ dɩm maama wʋnɩ, yɩ nmʋ pa bantʋ ŋwɩʋ maɩ dɩ dɩbam na joŋi te tɩn.›
translated: If you want to do something well, you must take your time. People who rush to do things quickly often make mistakes, but those who are patient and careful usually succeed in what they do.

ref: But Jesus called the disciples together and said: You know foreign rulers like to order their people around. And their great leaders have full power over everyone they rule.
text: Zezi laan ma bǝŋi ba maama, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam maŋɩ á ye lʋgʋ baŋa pwa na yǝni ba jɩgɩ ba nɔɔna ba bɛɛsɩ te tɩn, yɩ ba dɩdɛɛra mʋ di dam ba baŋa nɩ.
translated: Jesus answered them, “Truly, truly, I say to you, it was not Moses who gave you the bread from heaven, but my Father gives you the true bread from heaven.

ref: Two blind men were sitting beside the road. And when they heard that Jesus was coming their way, they shouted, “Lord and Son of David, have pity on us!”
text: Lilwǝ bale dɛɛn ma je cwǝŋǝ kam ni nɩ. Ba na lwarɩ nɩ Zezi mʋ tɔgɩ dáanɩ o maa kɛ tɩn, ba laan ma kaasɩ baŋa baŋa dɩ kwǝr-dɩa ba wɩ: «Dɩ́ Yuutu! Pɛ Davidi dwi tu! Duri dɩbam yibwǝnǝ!»
translated: The crowd went ahead of him and those who followed shouted, "Hosanna to the Son of David! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!"

ref: The disciples left and did what Jesus had told them to do.
text: Zezi karabiǝ bale bam ma sɩɩnɩ ba tɔgɩ yigǝ, ba vu ba kɩ kʋlʋ o na tagɩ sɩ ba kɩ tɩn.
translated: When the children see a snake, they run and climb a tree or call for someone to come and kill it.

ref: They brought the donkey and its colt and laid some clothes on their backs. Then Jesus got on.
text: Ba ma ja bɩna-kwɩʋn kʋm dɩ bɩna-pɔla kam ba ba, yɩ ba lɩ ba gwaarʋ ba daŋɩ sɩ baŋa nɩ. Zezi laan ma di bɩna-pɔla kam, yɩ ba ve ba maa kɛa.
translated: If someone wants to build a house and does not have money, he will go to his friends to ask for help. But if he does not have friends, he will not get any help.

ref: If you have faith when you pray, you will be given whatever you ask for.” A Question about Jesus' Authority (Mark
text: Kʋlʋ na wǝli da tɩn, abam na loori woŋo kʋlʋ maama Wɛ tee nɩ, yɩ á na kɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa nɩ á wʋ́ na, Wɛ wʋ́ pa á sɩɩnɩ á na-kʋ.» Nɔɔna na bwe Zezi dam dɩm na nuŋi mɛ tɩn (Marɩkɩ
translated: When evening came, the boat was already in the middle of the lake, and he was alone on land. He saw that they were making headway painfully, for the wind was against them. At about the fourth watch of the night he came to them, walking on the lake. (Mark)

ref: Who gave John the right to baptize? Was it God in heaven or merely some human being?” They thought it over and said to each other, “We can't say God gave John this right. Jesus will ask us why we didn't believe John.
text: A lagɩ a bwe abam a nii, Zan na miisi nɔɔna na wʋnɩ tɩn, kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ pɛ-o cwǝŋǝ naa nabiinǝ mʋ?» O na bwe-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, ba maa wʋra ba bwɛ daanɩ sɩ ba nii, ba na wʋ́ lǝr-o te tɩn. Ba maa wɩ: «Dɩbam na lǝri dɩ́ wɩ, kʋ yɩ Wɛ mʋ, o laan wʋ́ bwe dɩbam o nii, bɛɛ mʋ yɩ dɩ́ nan wʋ sɛ Zan taanɩ dɩm?
translated: When I was with you all, I asked you this question: “Who do people say that I am?” They answered me, saying that some say you are John the Baptist, others say you are Elijah, and still others say you are one of the prophets. Then I asked you: “But who do you say that I am?”

ref: The same thing happened to the second and third brothers and finally to all seven of them.
text: O dɩ ma tɩ o daar-o. Batɔ tu wʋm dɩ daa ma di-o. Kʋ kɩ kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ dɩ ba maama barpɛ, yɩ ba wʋ lʋgɩ bu dɩ kaanɩ wʋm.
translated: He went to his house. The dog followed him to his house. When he arrived there, he saw his mother sitting, and they greeted each other before he entered.

ref: Jesus answered: You are completely wrong! You don't know what the Scriptures teach. And you don't know anything about the power of God.
text: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam tiini á tusi mʋ. Abam nan wʋ ni Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm zaasɩm dɩm kuri dɩ, yɩ á yǝri Wɛ dam dɩm na yɩ te tɩn.
translated: Jesus answered him: “If you want to be perfect, go. Sell all you have and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come and follow me.”

ref: This is the first and most important commandment.
text: Dɩntʋ nan mʋ yɩ Wɛ dayigǝ ni yɩ dɩ yɩ kamunu.
translated: God loves us and He wants us to do good.

ref: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose family will he come from?” They answered, “He will be a son of King David.”
text: «Kʋ na yɩ Krisi wʋm Wɛ na lagɩ DƖ tʋŋɩ tɩn, abam bʋŋɩ nɩ oó ta yɩ wɔɔ dwi tu mʋ?» Ba ma lǝr-o ba wɩ: «Oó ta yɩ Pɛ Davidi dwi tu mʋ.»
translated: “Who is the Christ’s father? Whose son is he, according to what you know?” They answered him: “He is the son of David.”

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are show-offs, and you're in for trouble! You wash the outside of your cups and dishes, while inside there is nothing but greed and selfishness.
text: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ-ba dɩ Farizɩan tiinǝ-ba, lɛɛrʋ wʋ á yuu nɩ. Abam yɩ pipiri-nyɩna mʋ. Abam ŋwɩa kam nyɩ dɩ ba na sɩn zwɩ dɩ kabǝli kwɛ sɩ kʋ tɔgɩ cullu na brɩ te yɩ sɩ wʋ su dɩ digiru te tɩn mʋ. Abam wʋrʋ su dɩ kampinǝ dɩ pu-sɩŋa mʋ.
translated: Two men went up to the temple to pray, one a Pharisee and the other a tax collector. The Pharisee stood by himself and prayed: ‘God, I thank you that I am not like other people—robbers, evildoers, adulterers—or even like this tax collector. I fast twice a week and give a tenth of all I get.’

ref: And you claim you would not have taken part with your ancestors in killing the prophets.
text: Á ma daarɩ á tɛ á wɩ, abam dɛɛn ya na wʋra á kwǝ nakwa bam maŋa kam nɩ, á ya bá sɛ á wǝli dɩ ba á gʋ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ bam.
translated: If you do not do what I say, then you will not get what you want, and you will not be able to achieve your goals.

ref: I can promise that you people living today will be punished for all these things! Jesus Loves Jerusalem (Luke
text: A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, nɔɔna bantʋ maama caa wʋ́ ta tɔgɩ zɩm nɔɔna bam.» Zezi wʋ na cɔgɩ Zeruzalɛm tiinǝ ŋwaanɩ te tɩn (Luki
translated: When they were there, the time came for her to give birth, and she gave birth to her firstborn son. She wrapped him in cloths and placed him in a manger, because there was no room for them in the inn. Jesus was born in Bethlehem of Judea (Luke)

ref: Jesus replied, “Do you see these buildings? They will certainly be torn down! Not one stone will be left in place.” Warning about Trouble (Mark
text: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam wʋ nɛ ka na yɩ te tɩn na? A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, ka maama wʋ́ ba ka tʋ tɩga nɩ ka cɔgɩ maama. Kandwɛ daa bá daŋɩ dɩ doŋ baŋa nɩ.»
translated: He asked them: "Why are you looking for me? Didn’t you know I had to be in my Father’s house, doing my Father’s work?" But they did not understand what he was saying to them.

ref: Many false prophets will come and fool a lot of people.
text: Nɔɔna zanzan wʋ́ ba ba brɩ nɩ ba yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnǝ mʋ yɩ ba daɩ, yɩ baá ganɩ nɔɔna zanzan.
translated: If someone does not know what to do and you show them what to do, you have helped that person.

ref: They didn't know anything was happening until the flood came and swept them all away. This is how it will be when the Son of Man appears.
text: Ba dɛɛn wʋ lwarɩ woŋo kʋlʋ na lagɩ kʋ ba kʋ kɩ tɩn, sɩ kʋ vu kʋ yi maŋa kalʋ dʋ-fara kam na bam na tu ba li nɔɔna bam maama tɩn. Nabiin-bu wʋm dɩ lagɩ o ba kʋntʋ doŋ mʋ.
translated: If you want to get something good in life, you have to work hard for it, and you should not expect things to just come to you without effort. Even the Prophet worked hard and did not just sit and wait.

ref: Two men will be in the same field, but only one will be taken. The other will be left.
text: Kantʋ maŋa kam nɩ, nɔɔna bale na wʋ kara nɩ ba tʋŋa, Wɛ wʋ́ lɩ ba dɩdʋa DƖ daarɩ wʋdoŋ wʋm.
translated: If you do that, others will see you as a good person, and they will respect you everywhere you go.

ref: But suppose one of the servants thinks the master won't return until late.
text: O nan na yɩ tɩntʋŋ-balɔrɔ yɩ o bʋŋɩ nɩ, o yuutu wʋm daanɩ zanzan yɩ o ba tui,
translated: If you want to achieve something in life, you must work hard and not be lazy.

ref: If that happens, the master will surely come on a day and at a time when the servant least expects him.
text: tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm kʋntʋ yuutu wʋm laan wʋ́ joori o ba dɛ dɩm tɩntʋŋnʋ wʋm na ba bʋŋɩ nɩ oó ba tɩn, dɩ maŋa kalʋ o na wʋ́ ba o zʋ tɩn.
translated: The bird flies up and the hawk flies down and catches it, but if the bird is clever and escapes, the hawk will not get it.

ref: Those who were wise answered, “There's not enough oil for all of us! Go and buy some for yourselves.”
text: Swan tiinǝ bam ma lǝri ba wɩ: ‹Aye, nugǝ kam ba daga sɩ ka yi dɩbam maama. Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ, abam dɩ ve balʋ na jɩgɩ nugǝ ba yǝgi tɩn te á yǝgi.›
translated: The woman said to her child: ‘Yes, your father has gone to the farm with your uncle. When they return, I will give you the thing your father promised to give you.’

ref: Then the master said, “Now your money will be taken away and given to the servant with
text: Kʋntʋ tɩn, joŋi-na sǝbu-dala mʋrʋ dɩdʋa kam o tee nɩ á pa wʋlʋ na jɩgɩ sǝbu-dala mʋrr-fugǝ kam tɩn.
translated: Long ago, people used to gather under the big tree to listen to stories, and even today, children still gather under the same tree to listen to stories.

ref: Then the king will say to those on his right, “My father has blessed you! Come and receive the kingdom that was prepared for you before the world was created.
text: Nabiin-bu wʋm na yɩ pɛ kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan wʋ́ ta dɩ balʋ na wʋ o jazɩm nɩ tɩn o wɩ: ‹Abam balʋ a Ko Wɛ na kɩ lanyɩranɩ tɩn, á ba-na á tɔgɩ á di paarɩ dɩlʋ DƖ na maŋɩ DƖ tiŋi DƖ pa abam, kʋ na sɩŋɩ lʋgʋ pulim nɩ tɩn.
translated: When the prophet was sitting with his disciples, he lifted up his eyes and said to them: "If you have faith in God and do not doubt, you will not only do what was done to the fig tree, but even if you say to this mountain, 'Be lifted up and thrown into the sea,' it will happen."

ref: When I was hungry, you gave me something to eat, and when I was thirsty, you gave me something to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed me,
text: Bɛŋwaanɩ, kana dɛɛn jaanɩ amʋ, yɩ á pa-nɩ wʋdiu yɩ a di. Na-nyɔm dɛɛn jaanɩ-nɩ, yɩ á pa-nɩ na yɩ a nyɔ. A dɛɛn tu abam sam, yɩ á kɩ-nɩ voro lanyɩranɩ.
translated: First, if you see a person, you greet them and they respond. When you see another person, you greet them and they respond. If you see three people, you greet them all together.

ref: Then the king will say to those on his left, “Get away from me! You are under God's curse. Go into the everlasting fire prepared for the devil and his angels!
text: O laan ma wʋ́ daarɩ o ta dɩ balʋ na wʋ o jagwiǝ nɩ tɩn o wɩ: ‹Á ve daa a tee nɩ, sɩ abam mʋ yɩ balʋ Wɛ sɔɔla na wʋ á yuu nɩ tɩn. Kɛ-na á vu á zʋ mini dɩlʋ na ba dwe tɩn wʋ, Wɛ na maŋɩ DƖ tiŋi sɩ DƖ pa sʋtaanɩ dɩ dɩ malɛsɩ sɩm tɩn.
translated: He went and told him that he should not go there because he was afraid: "If you go there, you might not come back alive. But if you really want to go, then I cannot stop you. I have told you what I know and what I have heard from others, so now it is up to you to decide."

ref: I was hungry, but you did not give me anything to eat, and I was thirsty, but you did not give me anything to drink.
text: Bɛŋwaanɩ, kana dɛɛn jaanɩ amʋ, yɩ á wʋ pɛ-nɩ wʋdiu sɩ a di. Na-nyɔm dɩ ma ja-nɩ, yɩ á wʋ pɛ-nɩ na sɩ a nyɔ.
translated: First, if you want to eat, you must wash your hands before you eat. The same thing if you want to drink, you must wash your hands before you drink.

ref: Finally, Jesus returned to his disciples and said, “Are you still sleeping and resting? The time has come for the Son of Man to be handed over to sinners.
text: O daa ma joori o vu o karabiǝ bam te, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á ta tigi á dɔ mʋ na? Nii-na! Maŋa kam yiǝ, sɩ ba ja amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm ba kɩ nɔn-balwaarʋ jɩa nɩ.
translated: He took his money and went to the market, and when he got there he said: "Will you sell this to me? No! Go away, if you are not a Muslim you cannot enter this mosque."

ref: When Jesus was eating with his twelve disciples that evening, he said, “One of you will surely hand me over to my enemies.”
text: Ba na wʋra ba di tɩn, Zezi laan ma ta o wɩ: «A lagɩ a ta cɩga mʋ dɩ abam sɩ, abam wʋ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ lagɩ o yǝgi amʋ o pa nɔɔna.»
translated: No one lights a lamp and puts it under a basket, but they put it on a stand so that those who come in may see the light.

ref: During the meal Jesus took some bread in his hands. He blessed the bread and broke it. Then he gave it to his disciples and said, “Take this and eat it. This is my body.”
text: Ba na wʋra ba di maŋa kalʋ tɩn, Zezi ma kwe dɩpɛ yɩ o kɩ Wɛ le dɩ ŋwaanɩ. O ma daarɩ o fɔ-dɩ o pa o karabiǝ bam yɩ o wɩ: «Joŋi-na á di, sɩ dɩntʋ yɩ a yɩra mʋ.»
translated: When he had finished speaking, Jesus said to Simon, “Go out into the deep water, and let down your nets for a catch.”

ref: Jesus went with his disciples to a place called Gethsemane. When they got there, he told them, “Sit here while I go over there and pray.”
text: Zezi laan ma zaŋɩ o tɔgɩ dɩ o karabiǝ bam o vu jǝgǝ kadoŋ, gaarɩ na wʋra yɩ ba bǝ-ka nɩ Jesemanɩ tɩn. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á taá je yo, sɩ a vu daa a warɩ Wɛ sɩ a ba.»
translated: Jesus went and sat at the edge of the lake with his disciples, and many people came to him. He began to teach them, saying: "Listen, a sower went out to sow his seed."

ref: Judas had told them ahead of time, “Arrest the man I greet with a kiss.”
text: Zudasɩ ya maŋɩ o pa kɔgɔ kʋm lwarɩ nɩ, o na ve o kukwǝri nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ tɩn, sɩ ba ja kʋntʋ tu.
translated: When Judas saw that he had betrayed him, he was filled with regret, and he returned the thirty silver coins to the chief priests.

ref: and said, “This man claimed he could tear down God's temple and build it again in three days.”
text: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋntʋ tagɩ o wɩ, wʋm wʋ́ wanɩ o magɩ dɩbam Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm maama o dɩ tɩga nɩ, yɩ o joori o ma da yatɔ o lɔ-ka.»
translated: The child took his/her book and went, he/she said he/she was going to the market to buy something, and that he/she would return before it gets dark.

ref: “That is what you say!” Jesus answered. “But I tell all of you, ‘Soon you will see the Son of Man sitting at the right side of God All-Powerful and coming on the clouds of heaven.’ ”
text: Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Mʋ ka da n taga. Amʋ nan lagɩ a ta dɩ abam nɩ, abam wʋ́ ba á na Nabiin-bu wʋm o na je Dam-fɔrɔ Tu Baŋa-Wɛ jazɩm nɩ, yɩ á ta wʋ́ daarɩ á na-o o na nuŋi Wɛ-sɔŋɔ nɩ o maa bɩɩnɩ kunkwǝǝnu wʋnɩ.»
translated: Jesus answered him, “You have said so. But I tell you, from now on you will see the Son of Man seated at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven.”

ref: and Peter remembered that Jesus had said, “Before a rooster crows, you will say three times you don't know me.” Then Peter went out and cried bitterly.
text: Pɩyɛɛrɩ laan ma guli Zezi ya na tagɩ kʋlʋ dɩd-o o wɩ: «Kʋ na wʋ́ loori sɩ cɩbwɩʋ keeri tɩn, nmʋ wʋ́ fʋ kuni bɩtɔ nɩ n yǝri amʋ.» O na guli kʋntʋ tɩn, o laan ma nuŋi, yɩ o tiini o keeri zanzan.
translated: Jesus called his disciples and said to them, "I have compassion for these people; they have already been with me three days and have nothing to eat." He told the crowd to sit down, took the seven loaves, and gave thanks.

ref: Then they had a meeting and decided to buy a field that belonged to someone who made clay pots. They wanted to use it as a graveyard for foreigners.
text: Ba ma bwɛ daanɩ, yɩ ba ma-kʋ ba yǝgi tɩga kadoŋ, ka yɩrɩ mʋ Kamɔ-mɔrʋ tɩga, yɩ ba ma kɩ vǝrǝ yibeelǝ jǝgǝ.
translated: If we do this, we will be able to get good things, and with God's help, we will live in peace together.

ref: This is why people still call that place “Field of Blood.”
text: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ nɔɔna jɩgɩ jǝgǝ kam kʋntʋ ba ma bǝ nɩ Jana tɩga, sɩ kʋ ba kʋ yi zɩm.
translated: If someone wants to do something good but does not have the strength, and if he does not do it, it is not a sin.

ref: Jesus was brought before Pilate the governor, who asked him, “Are you the king of the Jews?” “Those are your words!” Jesus answered.
text: Zezi ma zaŋɩ o zɩgɩ gʋvɩrma tu Pilatɩ yigǝ nɩ. Gʋvɩrma tu wʋm ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ mʋ yɩ Zwifǝ bam pɛ wʋm na?» Zezi ma lǝr-o o wɩ: «Mʋ ka da n taga.»
translated: They brought Jesus and stood him before Pilate. Pilate asked him, "Are you the King of the Jews?" Jesus replied, "You have said so."

ref: But Jesus did not say anything, and the governor was greatly amazed. The Death Sentence (Mark
text: Zezi ma wʋ sɛ sɩ o lǝri ba taanɩ dɩm dɩlʋdɩlʋ. Kʋ ma pa gʋvɩrma tu wʋm tiini o yɩ yǝǝu dɩd-o.
translated: Jesus said that he is the good shepherd. He lays down his life for the sheep.

ref: They made a crown out of thorn branches and placed it on his head, and they put a stick in his right hand. The soldiers knelt down and pretended to worship him. They made fun of him and shouted, “Hey, you king of the Jews!”
text: Ba ma sɔ sabarɩ yipugǝ ba pu o yuu nɩ. Ba ma kwe miu ba ma ji nacǝgǝ ba kɩ o jazɩm nɩ. Ba laan ma yáal-o ba mwana, yɩ ba ve ba kuni doonǝ o yigǝ nɩ, yɩ ba tɛ ba wɩ: «Zwifǝ bam pɛ, dɩnle.»
translated: They did not wait for the morning before they left. They took their things and put them on their heads. When they reached the road, they saw someone coming towards them, and they said: "Let us ask him, maybe he knows."

ref: When the soldiers had finished making fun of Jesus, they took off the robe. They put his own clothes back on him and led him off to be nailed to a cross.
text: Ba na mwan-o ba ti tɩn, ba laan ma lɩ gwar-sʋŋʋ kʋm, yɩ ba daarɩ ba joori ba zʋ-o o tɩtɩ gwaarʋ tɩm. Ba laan ma ja-o ba nuŋi ba vu sɩ ba pa-o tʋʋn-dagara baŋa nɩ.
translated: Some people went to the farm, they found a big snake there, and they quickly ran back home to tell everyone. They said that those who are brave should come and help them kill the dangerous snake.

ref: Then about that time Jesus shouted, “Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?” which means, “My God, my God, why have you deserted me?”
text: kʋ kɩ nɩnɛɛnɩ luu batɔ mʋ te. Maŋa kam kʋntʋ nɩ Zezi laan ma kaasɩ baŋa baŋa dɩ kwǝr-dɩa o wɩ: «Eli, Eli, lɛma sabakɩtani?» Taanɩ dɩntʋ kuri mʋ: «Amʋ Wɛ, amʋ Wɛ, bɛɛ mʋ kɩ yɩ n yagɩ amʋ?»
translated: He cried out in a loud voice. Then Jesus shouted again in a loud voice and said: "Eli, Eli, lema sabachthani?" That means: "My God, my God, why have you abandoned me?"

ref: Many women had come with Jesus from Galilee to be of help to him, and they were there, looking on at a distance.
text: Kaana zanzan dɩ tɔgɩ ba wʋra, ba ma zɩgɩ yigǝ yigǝ yɩ ba niǝ. Bantʋ dɛɛn ya tɔgɩ dɩ Zezi mʋ ba nuŋi Galile ba ba, yɩ ba yǝni ba tɔgɩ ba zǝn-o.
translated: When the sun was rising, the women took the spices they had prepared and went to the tomb. They found the stone rolled away from the tomb, but when they entered, they did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.

ref: Then Jesus said, “Don't be afraid! Tell my followers to go to Galilee. They will see me there.” Report of the Guard
text: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Á yɩ taá kwarɩ fʋʋnɩ. Ve-na a ko-biǝ bam te, sɩ á ta dɩ ba sɩ ba vu Galile, sɩ dáanɩ mʋ baá na amʋ.»
translated: Jesus said to them: “You will all fall away. For it is written, I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep will be scattered.”

ref: Suddenly Jesus met them and greeted them. They went near him, held on to his feet, and worshiped him.
text: Ba na maa kɛ tɩn, Zezi ma da o jeeri-ba, yɩ o kɩ-ba le. Ba ma vu ba yi o te, ba kuni doonǝ ba ja o nɛ yɩ ba zul-o.
translated: If you do this, God will bless you, and you will not lack anything. If you do not do it, you will miss your reward, and your enemies will overcome you.

ref: Jesus came to them and said: I have been given all authority in heaven and on earth!
text: Zezi laan ma vu o yi ba te, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Wɛ pɛ-nɩ dam sɩ a taa te wɛyuu dɩ tɩga baŋa wǝǝnu maama.
translated: Jesus answered him, "Truly I say to you, today you will be with me in paradise."

ref: They saw him and worshiped him, but some of them doubted.
text: Ba ma sɩɩnɩ ba vu ba na-o, yɩ ba kuni doonǝ ba zul-o. Badaara maa ta jɩgɩ bʋbwɛa dɩd-o.
translated: If someone does not have something, and they see another person with it, they should not be jealous. Everyone has their own time for good things.

ref: Right then the two brothers dropped their nets and went with him.
text: O na tagɩ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma zaŋɩ lɩla ba yagɩ ba bʋrɩ sɩm, yɩ ba vu ba tɔgɩ o kwaga.

text: Nɔɔna balʋ maama na zʋʋrɩ Galile je sɩm nɩ tɩn dɛɛn ma ni Zezi ŋwa lɩla. Zezi na pɛ yawɩɩna zanzan na yazurǝ te tɩn (Matiyu
translated: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea, during the time of King Herod, wise men from the east came to Jerusalem (Matthew)

text: O maa tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ dɩ nɔɔna bam o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋdoŋ wʋ́ saŋɩ amʋ kwaga o ba, wʋntʋ tiini o dwe amʋ. A wʋ maŋɩ sɩ a tiiri a lɩ o nɛ natra a pa-o.

text: Zezi ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Zaŋɩ-na sɩ dɩ́ kɛ dɩ́ vu je sɩdonnǝ. A maŋɩ sɩ a vu tɩ-balwa balʋ na wʋ yoba seeni tɩn a tɔɔlɩ Wɛ kwǝrǝ kam. Kʋ yɩ kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ mʋ a nuŋi Wɛ te a ba.»
translated: Jesus answered him: "Truly I say to you, today you will be with me in Paradise."

text: Ba zanzan ma ba ba la daanɩ sɔŋɔ kʋm nɩ, yɩ laŋa daa tǝrǝ kʋ ni nɩ dɩ fɩnfɩɩn dɩ. O maa wʋra o ŋɔɔnɩ Wɛ taanɩ o brɩ-ba.

translated: Jesus went to the town of Levi and saw a man sitting at his tax booth. He said to him, "Follow me," and the man got up, left everything, and followed him.

text: Wɛ cullu karanyɩna tiinǝ badaara na nuŋi Farizɩan tiinǝ kɔgɔ kʋm wʋ tɩn maa tɔgɩ ba wʋra. Ba na nɛ Zezi na je o di wʋdiu dɩ nɔn-cɩcɔgɩrʋ dɩ lampo-joŋnǝ kʋntʋ tɩn, ba ma bwe o karabiǝ bam ba wɩ: «Bɛɛ mʋ kɩ yɩ o di wʋdiu dɩdaanɩ lampo-joŋnǝ dɩ nɔn-cɩcɔgɩrʋ tɩm kʋntʋ?»

text: Kʋntʋ ŋwaanɩ amʋ Nabiin-bu wʋm jɩgɩ ni siun dɛ cullu tɩm baŋa nɩ.»

ref: He even had to tell his disciples to get a boat ready to keep him from being crushed by the crowds.
text: Ba kɔgɔ kʋm na daga yɩ ba tiini ba pɩɩn-o zanzan tɩn, o ma ta dɩ o karabiǝ bam sɩ ba ja naboro ba ba ba zɩgɩ da, sɩ o wanɩ o zʋ kʋ wʋ.
translated: If someone eats and then goes to sleep immediately, it is not good for their body because the food will not digest well, and it can cause illness.

ref: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
text: Zezi laan ma bǝŋi-ba sɩ ba ba o te, yɩ o daarɩ o maŋɩ mɩmaŋa o brɩ-ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋ́ wanɩ o ma sʋtaana-pɛ dam o ma zǝli sʋtaana na?
translated: Jesus asked them, "Do you think it is right to heal on the Sabbath or not?"

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.

translated: Jesus was born in a small town. Many people did not know about his birth at that time. It was only later that Jesus began his work and many people started to hear about him and believe in him.

translated: The child took the food and ate it, but when he finished eating, he did not wash his hands. That is not good.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Kʋ daarɩ, wʋlʋ maama na ŋɔɔnɩ o twɩ Wɛ Joro kʋm tɩn, Wɛ bá sɛ sɩ DƖ ma cɛ kʋntʋ tu maŋa dɩ maŋa. Lwarɩm dɩm kʋntʋ wʋ́ ta tɔgɩ kʋntʋ tu sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kalʋ na ba ti tɩn.»
translated: In the morning, when the mother finishes pounding the millet, she says to her child, “Go and call your father so that he can come and eat with us.” The child goes to call his father, but on the way, he meets people and forgets to call his father as his mother told him.

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.

text: O na dugi-kʋ tɩn, kʋ laan ma nuŋi kʋ ji kamunu kʋ dwǝni dwǝ balʋ maama na wʋ o kara kam nɩ tɩn. Kʋ ma ta nɛ kamunǝ kamunǝ, yɩ zunǝ tui ba sɔ ba pwǝru kʋ yuu nɩ, kʋ na jɩgɩ woro tɩn ŋwaanɩ.»
translated: If you do good, good will come to you and if you do bad, bad will come to you. You should not do bad things, so that people will love you, and you will live a good life.

translated: Some people do not eat meat. Jesus said that we should love one another, and help each other in our needs.

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Zezi laan ma kaanɩ-ba o wɩ, ba yɩ zaŋɩ ba pa nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ lwarɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn. O ma daarɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ, ba kɩ wʋdiu ba pa bu wʋm sɩ o di.

text: O laan ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ na yɩ Wɛ nijoŋnu, nɔɔna yǝni ba pa-o zulǝ lanyɩranɩ, kʋ daarɩ o tɩtɩ tɩʋ tiinǝ dɩ o sɔŋɔ tiinǝ dɩ o cʋrrʋ nan bá nɩg-o.»

text: Zan karabiǝ bam na lwarɩ kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn, ba ma zaŋɩ ba vu ba kwe o yɩra yam ba ja vu ba kɩ. Zezi na pɛ kɔ-fɔrɔ di wʋdiu ba su te tɩn (Matiyu

translated: But if you do not do so, sin is crouching at your door; it desires to have you, but you must rule over it.

text: O daa ta ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam yǝni á vɩn Wɛ niǝ yam, yɩ á daarɩ á jɩgɩ á tɩtɩ wʋbʋŋa cullu mʋ á tɔga.
translated: He said to his child: "My child, if you go to the river, bring me a calabash full of clean water."

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.
text: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Abam yɩ pipiri-nyɩna mʋ. Wɛ nijoŋnu Ezayi dɛɛn jɩgɩ bʋra dɩ o na maŋɩ o ta abam taanɩ faŋa faŋa te tɩn. Kʋ pʋpʋnɩ Wɛ tɔnɔ kʋm wʋnɩ kʋ wɩ: ‹Nɔɔna bantʋ zuli amʋ Wɛ dɩ ba niǝ mʋ, yɩ ba wʋbʋŋa baŋwɛ dɩ amʋ.
translated: He said to me: “This message is for all people. As the prophet Isaiah wrote long ago, someone will come to prepare the way for the Lord. He will shout in the desert: ‘Get the road ready for the Lord to come, make the paths straight for him.’”

ref: He answered, “Don't you know what I am talking about by now? You surely know that the food you put into your mouth cannot make you unclean.
text: O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam dɩ ba jɩgɩ wʋbʋŋa na? Á maŋɩ sɩ á taá ye nɩ, woŋo kʋlʋ maama nɔɔnʋ na wʋ́ kwe o pa kʋ tɔgɩ o ni kʋ zʋ o wʋ tɩn bá wanɩ kʋ cɔg-o.
translated: He called them and said: "Why are you standing here idle all day? If you want to work, go into my vineyard and I will pay you what is right."

ref: When the woman got back home, she found her child lying on the bed. The demon had gone. Jesus Heals a Man Who Was Deaf and Could Hardly Talk
text: Kaanɩ wʋm ma sɩɩnɩ o joori o vu sɔŋɔ. O ma na o bukɔ wʋm o tigi o tigǝ je nɩ, dɩ cicirǝ kam nuŋi ka daar-o. Zezi na pɛ zwa-kɔgɔ na yɩ bibǝku na yazurǝ te tɩn
translated: The man went to the market to buy some food. He saw his friend there and they talked for a while. Later, they both returned home together in the evening.

ref: Jesus left the region around Tyre and went by way of Sidon toward Lake Galilee. He went through the land near the ten cities known as Decapolis.
text: Zezi laan ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi Tiiri nawurǝ yam nɩ o da Sidɔn o kɛ. O laan ma tɔgɩ je sɩlʋ ba na bǝ nɩ Tɩɩnɩ Fugǝ tɩn o vu o yi Galile nɩnɩʋ kʋm je sɩm.
translated: Jesus left that place and went to the region of Tyre. He entered a house and did not want anyone to know it; yet he could not keep his presence secret.

ref: Jesus told the people not to say anything about what he had done. But the more he told them, the more they talked about it.
text: Zezi ma kaanɩ nɔɔna bam maama, sɩ ba yɩ ta kʋlʋ na kɩ tɩn ba brɩ nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ. O na kaanɩ-ba kʋntʋ tɩn, mʋ ba laan fɔgɩ ba tɛ dɩ nɔɔna bam.
translated: If someone does not respect their parents, they will not grow well and will have many problems. But if they respect them, then they will receive blessings and live a good life.

ref: Then Jesus explained clearly what he meant. Peter took Jesus aside and told him to stop talking like that.
text: O maa ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ jaja o brɩ-ba. Pɩyɛɛrɩ ma ja-o o vu daa, yɩ o kaan-o o wɩ, o yɩ taa ŋɔɔnɩ kʋntʋ doŋ.
translated: He/she/they went to the farm early in the morning. When he/she/they arrived there, he/she/they started working, and he/she/they stayed on the farm until evening.

ref: Jesus took him by the hand and led him out of the village, where he spit into the man's eyes. He placed his hands on the blind man and asked him if he could see anything.
text: O ma ja lilwe wʋm jɩŋa o vaŋɩ o ja nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm daa. O ma twɩ lileeru o kɩ o yiǝ nɩ, yɩ o daŋɩ o jɩa lilwe wʋm baŋa nɩ. O laan ma bwe-o o wɩ: «Nmʋ naɩ woŋo na?»
translated: He took the calabash and went to the river to fetch water. He filled the calabash and brought it back, but when he arrived, the calabash was empty again. He then asked himself: "What is happening to me?"

ref: But when Jesus turned and saw the disciples, he corrected Peter. He said to him, “Satan, get away from me! You are thinking like everyone else and not like God.”
text: Zezi ma guni o nii o karabiǝ bam seeni, yɩ o bagɩ Pɩyɛɛrɩ o wɩ: «Sʋtaanɩ, ve daa a tee nɩ! Nmʋ wʋbʋŋa yam yɩ nabiinǝ nyɩm mʋ, sɩ ya ba tɔgɩ Wɛ na lagɩ te tɩn.»
translated: Jesus went up the mountain and called those he wanted, and they came to him. He appointed twelve, whom he also named apostles, to be with him and to be sent out to preach.

ref: Jesus then told the crowd and the disciples to come closer, and he said: If any of you want to be my followers, you must forget about yourself. You must take up your cross and follow me.
text: Zezi laan ma bǝŋi nɔn-kɔgɔ kʋm maama dɩ o karabiǝ bam sɩ ba ba o te. O ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Nɔɔnʋ wʋlʋ maama na lagɩ sɩ o taa tɔgɩ amʋ kwaga tɩn, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o vɩn o tɩtɩ wʋbʋŋa sɩ o sɛ yaara nɩ wʋlʋ na zɩŋɩ tʋʋn-dagara o maa ve o tʋʋnɩ jǝgǝ te tɩn, sɩ o laan taa tɔgɩ-nɩ.
translated: Jesus said to his disciples that he would not leave them alone. He told them: "Soon the world will not see me anymore, but you will see me, because I live and you also will live. On that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you are in me, and I am in you."

ref: If you want to save your life, you will destroy it. But if you give up your life for me and for the good news, you will save it.
text: Wʋlʋ maama nan na lagɩ sɩ o yɩrɩ o ŋwɩa tɩn, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ ga-ka mʋ. Kʋ daarɩ, wʋlʋ maama na sɛ sɩ o ga o ŋwɩa amʋ ŋwaanɩ dɩ Wɛ kwǝr-ywǝŋǝ kam ŋwaanɩ, kʋntʋ tu wʋ́ na ŋwɩa Wɛ tee nɩ.
translated: When a woman gives birth and her child does not survive, people say she is unlucky. But if the woman gives birth and her child survives and grows up to become a chief, people say she has given birth to a great person.

ref: After Jesus and the disciples had gone back home and were alone, they asked him, “Why couldn't we force out that demon?”
text: Zezi na joori o zʋ digǝ tɩn, o karabiǝ bam ma vu o te ba yɩranɩ, yɩ ba bwe-o ba wɩ: «Bɛŋwaanɩ mʋ dɩbam wʋ wanɩ dɩ́ zǝli cicirǝ kam?»
translated: Jesus was sitting at home, and his disciples came to him and asked him, saying: "Teacher, why do you always speak to the people in parables?"

ref: Jesus said, “You people don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy to me.”
text: Zezi na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, o ma ta o wɩ: «Abam zɩm nɔɔna bam brɩ á ba jɩgɩ wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ. Amʋ wʋ́ daanɩ abam tɩtarɩ nɩ a kɩ wʋ-zuru dɩ abam sɩ kʋ taa ve maŋa kɔɔ? Ja-na bu wʋm á ba.»
translated: Jesus said to him, "Foxes have holes and birds of the air have nests, but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head."

ref: But Jesus took hold of his hand and helped him stand up.
text: Zezi laan ma ja o jɩŋa o pa o zaŋɩ wɛɛnɩ o zɩgɩ.
translated: Jesus said that we should love one another.

ref: Jesus answered, “Only prayer can force out this kind of demon.” Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Matthew
text: O ma lǝri-ba o wɩ: «Cicirǝ kantʋ doŋ zǝlim cana. Cicirǝ kantʋ dwi warɩ ka zǝli kʋ na daɩ dɩ Wɛ-loro dam.»
translated: He/she told him/her: "The bird has already eaten the millet. The bird will come again if you do not guard the millet at the Wɛ-loro farm."

ref: Jesus and his disciples went to his home in Capernaum. After they were inside the house, Jesus asked them, “What were you arguing about along the way?”
text: Ba ma vu ba yi Kapɛrnawum. Ba ma zʋ sɔŋɔ. Zezi ma bwe o karabiǝ bam o wɩ: «Dɩ́ na wʋ cwǝŋǝ nɩ dɩ́ maa bɩɩnɩ tɩn, á ya magɩ kantɔgɔ bɛɛ ŋwaanɩ mʋ?»
translated: They went down to Capernaum. They stayed there. On the Sabbath, Jesus began to teach the people. He said: "Why are you troubled and why do doubts arise in your hearts?"

ref: After Jesus left, he went to Judea and then on to the other side of the Jordan River. Once again large crowds came to him, and as usual, he taught them.
text: Zezi laan ma zaŋɩ o nuŋi je sɩm kʋntʋ nɩ o vu o da Zude tɩ-niǝ yam. O ma vu o bɛ Zʋrdɛn bugǝ kam ni dɩdoŋ dɩm nɩ. Nɔn-kɔgɔ zanzan daa ma ba ba gilim-o. O ma ta brɩ-ba Wɛ cɩga kam, nɩ o na yǝni o kɩ te tɩn.
translated: After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea, during the time of King Herod, Magi from the east came to Jerusalem and asked, "Where is the one who has been born king of the Jews? We saw his star when it rose and have come to worship him."

ref: He becomes like one person with his wife. Then they are no longer two people, but one.
text: Kʋntʋ wʋ́ pa ba laan ji nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa. Ba daa daɩ nɔɔna bale, ba laan yɩ nɔɔnʋ dɩdʋa mʋ.
translated: When someone is in trouble, they need help. If they do not get help, they remain in trouble.

ref: And no one should separate a couple that God has joined together.”
text: Wɛ na kɩ nɔɔna balʋ daanɩ tɩn, nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa wʋ maŋɩ sɩ o pɔɔrɩ-ba daanɩ.»
translated: If you give a child a name, the child will always remember who gave him that name.

ref: When Jesus and his disciples were back in the house, they asked him about what he had said.
text: Zezi dɩ o karabiǝ bam laan ma joori ba vu sɔŋɔ. Ba na zʋ tɩn, ba ma bwe-o o taanɩ dɩm kuri.
translated: Yesterday he went to the farm with his friends to work. They returned late, and his mother was worried about him.

ref: Some people brought their children to Jesus so he could bless them by placing his hands on them. But his disciples told the people to stop bothering him.
text: Nɔɔna badonnǝ maa jaanɩ biǝ ba tui Zezi te sɩ o daŋɩ o jɩa ba baŋa nɩ. O karabiǝ bam ma zaŋɩ ba bagɩ-ba sɩ ba yɩ taa kɩ kʋntʋ.
translated: Some women went to the tomb early in the morning and they saw that the stone had been rolled away. They entered but did not find the body of the Lord Jesus.

ref: Jesus looked around and said to his disciples, “It's hard for rich people to get into God's kingdom!”
text: Zezi laan ma pipiri o nii o karabiǝ bam seeni, yɩ o ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Nadunǝ na lagɩ sɩ ba zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ, kʋʋ́ ta tiini kʋ cana kʋ pa-ba.»
translated: Jesus called the children to him and said, "Let the little children come to me, and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of God belongs to such as these."

ref: The disciples were shocked to hear this. So Jesus told them again, “It's terribly hard to get into God's kingdom!
text: O karabiǝ bam na ni kʋntʋ tɩn, kʋ ma sʋ-ba. Zezi daa ta ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «A biǝ-ba, nɔɔna na lagɩ sɩ ba zʋ Wɛ paarɩ dɩm wʋ, kʋʋ́ ta tiini kʋ cana kʋ pa-ba.
translated: When the child was still very young, he could not walk. One day his father called him and said: "My child, come here and let me see if you can walk to me, so that I know you can walk."

ref: Jesus told them, “You don't really know what you're asking! Are you able to drink from the cup that I must soon drink from or be baptized as I must be baptized?”
text: Zezi ma ta dɩ ba o wɩ: «Abam tɩtɩ yǝri á na loori kʋlʋ tɩn. Abam wʋ́ wanɩ á tɔgɩ á yaarɩ, nɩ amʋ na lagɩ a ba a na yaara yalʋ tɩn na? Á wʋ́ wanɩ á yaarɩ á tɩ, nɩ a na lagɩ a yaarɩ a tɩ te tɩn na?»
translated: Jesus said to them: "Suppose one of you has a hundred sheep and loses one of them. Does he not leave the ninety-nine in the open country and go after the lost sheep until he finds it? And when he finds it, does he not joyfully put it on his shoulders and go home?"

ref: And if you want to be first, you must be everyone's slave.
text: Wʋlʋ na lagɩ sɩ o ji abam yigǝ tu tɩn, kʋntʋ tu maŋɩ sɩ o ji abam maama gambaa.
translated: If someone gives you a gift with one hand, be careful if they give you a gift with both hands.

ref: Many people told the man to stop, but he shouted even louder, “Son of David, have pity on me!”
text: Nɔɔna zanzan ma bag-o sɩ o pu o ni. O laan ma tiini o kaasɩ zanzan yɩ o wɩ: «Pɛ Davidi dwi tu, duri a yibwǝnǝ!»
translated: The woman said to her child that she would give him food. She then called her eldest son and said to him: "Go and call David, tell him to come quickly!"

ref: Jesus told him, “You may go. Your eyes are healed because of your faith.” At once the man could see, and he went down the road with Jesus.
text: Zezi ma ta dɩd-o o wɩ: «Nan ve, nmʋ na kɩ n wʋ-dɩdʋa dɩ amʋ te tɩn mʋ pɛ n na yazurǝ.» Zezi na tagɩ kʋntʋ bɩdwɩ baŋa nɩ tɩn, mʋ lilwe wʋm yiǝ joori ya naɩ. O maa tɔgɩ Zezi kwaga cwǝŋǝ kam nɩ.
translated: Jesus sat down and called the twelve and said to them: "If anyone wants to be first, he must be the very last and the servant of all." Jesus took a little child and had him stand among them, and taking him in his arms, he said to them. He spoke these words to his disciples.

ref: After Jesus had gone to Jerusalem, he went into the temple and looked around at everything. But since it was already late in the day, he went back to Bethany with the twelve disciples. Jesus Puts a Curse on a Fig Tree (Matthew
text: Zezi ma vu o zʋ Zeruzalɛm tɩʋ kʋm wʋ. O ma vu o yi Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm o zʋ kʋ wʋ. O ma kaagɩ o nii wǝǝnu tɩm maama na yɩ te. Wɛ na lagɩ dɩ zʋ tɩn, o ma joori o nuŋi tɩʋ kʋm wʋnɩ, o tɔgɩ dɩ o karabiǝ fugǝ-bale bam o vu Betani. Zezi na sɔɔlɩ kapʋrʋ te tɩn (Matiyu
translated: Jesus went to Jerusalem with his disciples. He went to the Mount of Olives with them. He told two of his followers to go ahead. When they reached the village, he told them they would find a donkey tied there, and he asked them to untie it and bring it to him at Bethany. Jesus did this to fulfill what was written (Matthew)

ref: If anyone asks why you are doing this, say, ‘The Lord needs it and will soon bring it back.’ ”
text: Nɔɔnʋ na bwe abam nɩ: ‹Bɛɛ mʋ yɩ á bwǝli-ka?›, sɩ á ta dɩd-o nɩ: ‹Kʋ yɩ dɩ́ Yuutu wʋm mʋ laga. Oó joori o pa ka ba lele.› »
translated: The child asked his father: "Where are you going this morning?", and he replied: "I am going to Yuutu to buy some millet. Your mother said there is none left."

ref: The disciples led the donkey to Jesus. They put some of their clothes on its back, and Jesus got on.
text: Ba ma ja bɩna-pɔla kam ba ja vu ba yi Zezi te. Ba laan ma kwe ba gwaarʋ ba daŋɩ ka baŋa nɩ. Zezi ma di ka baŋa, yɩ ba ve ba maa kɛa.
translated: They took Jesus to the place called Golgotha. They gave him wine mixed with myrrh, but he did not take it. Then they crucified him, and divided his clothes among themselves.

ref: God bless the coming kingdom of our ancestor David. Hooray for God in heaven above!”
text: Zuli-na wʋlʋ na wʋ́ ta te o nɔɔna, nɩnɛɛnɩ dɩbam ko Davidi dɛɛn na jɩgɩ paarɩ te tɩn. Ozaana! Zulǝ taa wʋ Baŋa-Wɛ tee nɩ!»
translated: They spread their clothes on the road as he passed by, and the people shouted with a loud voice because of all the miracles they had seen: Hosanna! Blessed is he who comes in the name of the Lord!

ref: So Jesus said to the tree, “Never again will anyone eat fruit from this tree!” The disciples heard him say this. Jesus in the Temple (Matthew
text: O laan ma ta dɩ kapʋrʋ kʋm o wɩ: «Nɔɔn-nɔɔnʋ daa bá na mɔɔla nmʋ yuu nɩ o di.» O karabiǝ bam ma ni o na tagɩ kʋlʋ tɩn. Zezi na zǝli pipimpiinǝ Wɛ-di-kamunu kʋm wʋnɩ te tɩn (Matiyu
translated: He went and called his servants and said: “Each of you has been given some money to trade with.” He gave them to each according to his ability. This is part of the parable of the Talents (Matthew).

ref: Jesus would not let anyone carry things through the temple.
text: O ma wʋ sɛ sɩ nɔɔna taa zɩŋɩ ba zɩla ba tɔgɩ kʋnkɔlɔ kʋm wʋ ba kɛa.
translated: He said that if a child does not listen to advice, he will face difficulties in life.
